description: RGB shows a dense urban core with road networks and building blocks; SWIR confirms the urban footprint in magenta/pink tones surrounded by healthy bright-green vegetation with no dark water pooling visible.
labeler_notes: Post-flood timestamp but no residual inundation detectable in either band; urban scene only.

description: RGB shows a forested hillside transitioning to a suburban fringe with dense green canopy dominating; SWIR confirms bright-green healthy vegetation across the frame with no dark water bodies or anomalous signatures.
labeler_notes: Well past the May 8 event peak; vegetation appears fully healthy with no flood aftermath detectable.

description: RGB shows a mid-size city surrounded by agricultural fields with a river at normal levels; SWIR shows yellow/orange-dominant agricultural land and pink urban area with no dark water pooling — a pre-flood baseline.
labeler_notes: Capture is one week before the Sep 8 flood peak; no hazard visible, useful as baseline reference.

description: RGB shows flat agricultural patchwork fields with a small settlement and a river at normal levels; SWIR shows a yellow/green field mosaic with healthy crops and no inundation signatures.
labeler_notes: Pre-event baseline for the agricultural tile; useful for change detection comparison against peak and recovery imagery.

description: RGB is nearly entirely black with only a thin white strip at top; SWIR similarly shows a thin light cyan strip and massive black no-data region covering ~90% of the frame.
labeler_notes: Acquisition at the flood peak is almost entirely obscured — likely cloud shadow + no-data artifact; not usable for labeling.

description: RGB is completely white indicating total cloud cover; SWIR is uniformly light cyan/blue confirming full cloud obscuration with no ground signal.
labeler_notes: 100% cloud cover on both bands; entirely unusable for hazard assessment.

description: RGB shows the city with visibly dark patches along the river corridor and in adjacent agricultural areas consistent with residual flooding; SWIR confirms dark blue/black water signatures in multiple areas flanking the urban zone.
labeler_notes: 12 days after the Sep 8 peak; clear residual floodwater still present in SWIR, makes this a strong HIGH for flood aftermath.

description: RGB shows agricultural fields with scattered dark wet patches and a small settlement; SWIR reveals dark blue/black pooling distributed across multiple field parcels and along the river margin confirming residual inundation.
labeler_notes: Agricultural tile shows clear multi-field inundation still present in SWIR 12 days post-peak; good HIGH training example.

description: RGB shows the city with a river that appears wider and darker than the pre-event baseline, suggesting elevated water levels; SWIR shows a dark blue water signature along the river and scattered dark patches in surrounding fields, indicating partial but incomplete flood recession.
labeler_notes: 37 days after peak, river and some periurban areas still show water presence in SWIR; borderline HIGH/MEDIUM — keeping HIGH given confirmed water above baseline.

description: RGB shows agricultural fields that are largely recovering but with some anomalous dark/wet-looking parcels; SWIR shows bright-green recovered vegetation dominating but with a distinct dark blue water body along the river margin and residual dark patches in low-lying field areas.
labeler_notes: Late recovery stage — most fields have recovered but river-adjacent areas retain water signal in SWIR; labeled HIGH due to confirmed residual water.
